text:
| document_explanation__non_molestation_order__cross_cutting | cross_cutting | What unrepresented people most commonly get wrong when applying for a non-molestation order — e.g. insufficient detail in supporting statements, misunderstanding the without notice threshold, not being aware of legal aid availability, or not knowing what evidence is needed to satisfy the legal aid evidence requirement |
| legal_principle__proportionality__cross_cutting | cross_cutting | How unrepresented people experience the proportionality principle in practice — e.g. whether they feel shut down when they try to raise issues, or whether they are aware the court has discretion to limit what is heard |
| legal_principle__without_prejudice__cross_cutting | cross_cutting | Whether unrepresented people understand the without prejudice rule at all, and whether they have made mistakes — e.g. referring to without prejudice offers in open correspondence or in statements — that have caused problems in their proceedings |
| process_explanation__child_arrangements_final_hearing__child_arrangements | child_arrangements | What unrepresented people typically get wrong at the final hearing — e.g. not understanding the structure of the hearing, attempting to introduce new evidence not directed by the court, not preparing cross-examination questions in advance, misunderstanding what the judge's questions mean, not bringing the bundle or not preparing a bundle correctly |
